proved: A version envelope has an exact schema, project identity, parent hash, structured semantic policy blocks, sorted unique scenario references, participant ID, display-name-at-save, timestamp, and optional note.
Canonical envelopes are stored as strings under their SHA-256 address and every read reparses, re-canonicalizes, and rehashes before returning data.
Replacing stored content without changing the key is detected and fails closed; callers receive detached blocks, scenario IDs, and attribution objects.
Repeating the identical save is idempotent and retains one record.
A child requires a valid parent from the same project; parent lineage is part of the hashed envelope.
Historical versions retain a stable participant ID and the display name recorded at save time when a later version uses a different display name.
Two peers independently creating children from one root converge to all three valid versions across forty reordered and duplicate update deliveries.
P-23 and P-27 are implemented_unverified; no save/restore UI, shared head, diff, archive, authentication, or remote-transport claim is made.
notProved: A user can save, browse, label, compare, or restore versions in the product UI.
Restore creates a new head, or any shared head-selection conflict policy.
Deterministic change notes, policy diffs, scenario reruns, evaluations, or exports.
Participant identity is authenticated or portable between installations.
Two-machine remote collaboration, repair of malicious replacement, signed history, or portable archive verification.
Semantic quality or completeness of any saved policy.
